result.markdown:
--- title: Identifying model
misspecifications author: W. Jake Thompson, Ph.D. html-table-processing: none --- ## Types of misspecifications * Attributes * How many, and which ones? * Items * Alignment to attributes * Measurement model # Attribute misspecifications ## Attribute identification * Have we identified the correct number of attributes? * Ideally, this is a content-driven process from the beginning * Identify the skills or constructs we want to measure * Develop items specifically for those skills using principles of evidence centered design * But often we're handed data, or are attempting to retrofit a DCM to a previously developed assessment ## Too many attributes {.exercise} * What might some indicators be that we have included too many attributes in our model? :::{.fragment} * Some we'll examine today: * Structural parameters: Are there classes that aren't observed and aren't explained by a hierarchy? * Attribute correlations: If attributes are very highly correlated, are they really measuring distinct skills? ::: ## Structural parameters ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-line-numbers="|5,8,11,12"} measr_extract(ecpe_lcdm, "strc_param") #> # A tibble: 8 × 5 #> class morphosyntactic cohesive lexical estimate #> #> 1 [0,0,0] 0 0 0 0.291 ± 0.00588 #> 2 [1,0,0] 1 0 0 0.018 ± 0.00198 #> 3 [0,1,0] 0 1 0 0.020 ± 0.00117 #> 4 [0,0,1] 0 0 1 0.133 ± 0.00517 #> 5 [1,1,0] 1 1 0 0.015 ± 0.00253 #> 6 [1,0,1] 1 0 1 0.020 ± 0.00097 #> 7 [0,1,1] 0 1 1 0.154 ± 0.00909 #> 8 [1,1,1] 1 1 1 0.350 ± 0.00487 ``` ::: * We see respondents in all of the expected classes ## Attribute correlations ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} attribute_corr(ecpe_lcdm) #> morphosyntactic cohesive lexical #> morphosyntactic 1.0000000 0.8978362 0.7808127 #> cohesive 0.8978362 1.0000000 0.9297594 #> lexical 0.7808127 0.9297594 1.0000000 ``` ::: * We expect to see somewhat high correlations (but not perfect) due to the attribute dependencies ## Exercise 1 {.exercise} * Open `misspecifications.Rmd` and run the `setup` chunk. * Examine the base rates and attribute correlations for the PIE HDCM model. * What do the findings indicate? {{< countdown "4:00" bottom="15%" >}} ## {.exercise data-menu-title="Solution 1"} ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ::: :::{.panel-tabset} ### Structural parameters ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-line-numbers="|6"} measr_extract(pie_hdcm, "strc_param") #> # A tibble: 4 × 5 #> class L1 L2 L3 estimate #> #> 1 [0,0,0] 0 0 0 0.047 ± 0.036 #> 2 [1,0,0] 1 0 0 0.055 ± 0.056 #> 3 [1,1,0] 1 1 0 0.551 ± 0.080 #> 4 [1,1,1] 1 1 1 0.347 ± 0.051 ``` ::: * Very few students are proficient on only Level 1 skills ### Correlations ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} attribute_corr(pie_hdcm) #> L1 L2 L3 #> L1 1.000000000000 0.8703714 -0.000002912461 #> L2 0.870371441323 1.0000000 0.251065826627 #> L3 -0.000002912461 0.2510658 1.000000000000 ``` ::: * Correlation between Levels 1 and 2 is much higher than between Levels 2 and 3 ::: ## Too few attributes {.exercise} * What might some indicators be that we have included too few attributes in our model? :::{.fragment} * Some we'll examine today: * Local independence: Item responses should be independent conditional on the respondent's profile of proficiency ::: ## Residual correlations with Yen's Q~3~ * Residual correlations should all be relatively small (e.g., <.2) :::{.new-height style="--code-height: 420px;"} ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} yens_q3(ecpe_lcdm) #> # A tibble: 378 × 4 #> item_1 item_2 resid_corr flag #> #> 1 E1 E2 0.00496 FALSE #> 2 E1 E3 -0.0332 FALSE #> 3 E1 E4 0.0125 FALSE #> 4 E1 E5 0.0213 FALSE #> 5 E1 E6 0.0181 FALSE #> 6 E1 E7 0.000101 FALSE #> 7 E1 E8 -0.0240 FALSE #> 8 E1 E9 -0.0341 FALSE #> 9 E1 E10 -0.00660 FALSE #> 10 E1 E11 -0.0262 FALSE #> # ℹ 368 more rows ``` ::: ::: ## Visualizing Yen's Q~3~ ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](02-advdcm-04-misspecifications-slides_files/figure-revealjs/vis-yensq3-1.png){fig-align='center' fig-alt='Histogram showing the distribution of residual correlations. All residual correlations are within the expected bounds of -.2 to .2.' width=90%} ::: ::: ## Exercise 2 {.exercise} * Calculate Yen's Q~3~ (residual correlations) for the PIE HDCM * What do the findings indicate? {{< countdown "2:00" bottom="15%" >}} ## {.exercise data-menu-title="Solution 2"} :::{.panel-tabset} ### Q~3~ distribution ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](02-advdcm-04-misspecifications-slides_files/figure-revealjs/vis-yensq3-pie-1.png){fig-align='center' fig-alt='Histogram showing the distribution of residual correlations. Several' width=90%} ::: ::: ### Flagged pairs ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} yens_q3(pie_hdcm) |> filter(flag) #> # A tibble: 11 × 4 #> item_1 item_2 resid_corr flag #> #> 1 00592 14415 0.209 TRUE #> 2 00592 06238 0.402 TRUE #> 3 00592 97634 -0.241 TRUE #> 4 14415 64967 0.208 TRUE #> 5 06238 97634 -0.215 TRUE #> 6 06238 27971 -0.238 TRUE #> 7 10231 97634 0.450 TRUE #> 8 54596 27971 0.213 TRUE #> 9 54596 63088 -0.246 TRUE #> 10 96748 56741 0.211 TRUE #> 11 13080 56741 0.257 TRUE ``` ::: ::: # Item misspecifications ## Diagnosing item-level problems * [Item-level fit](/materials/slides/01-introdcm-04-evaluation-slides.html#item-level-fit) with posterior predictive model checks * *p*-values * Conditional probabilities * Odds ratios * Item discrimination * Q-matrix validation ## Item discrimination * Evaluates how well an item is able to distinguish between attribute profiles * Item- and test-level discrimination ([Henson & Douglas, 2005](https://doi.org/10.1177/0146621604272623)) * Attribute-level discrimination ([Henson et al., 2008](https://doi.org/10.1177/0146621607302478)) * Higher is better, but no clear thresholds as values are dependent on the number of attributes, model that was estimated, etc. * Low values may indicate an item is not measuring what we intend (or not measuring well) ## CDI with measr ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-line-numbers="|8|22"} cdi(ecpe_lcdm) #> $item_discrimination #> # A tibble: 28 × 5 #> item_id overall morphosyntactic cohesive lexical #> #> 1 E1 0.0685 0.0639 0.0497 0 #> 2 E2 0.0491 0 0.102 0 #> 3 E3 0.0956 0.144 0 0.0400 #> 4 E4 0.141 0 0 0.293 #> 5 E5 0.0908 0 0 0.188 #> 6 E6 0.0750 0 0 0.155 #> 7 E7 0.247 0.383 0 0.0797 #> 8 E8 0.0633 0 0.131 0 #> 9 E9 0.0920 0 0 0.191 #> 10 E10 0.201 0.416 0 0 #> # ℹ 18 more rows #> #> $test_discrimination #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #> overall morphosyntactic cohesive lexical #> #> 1 3.58 2.70 0.962 3.48 ``` ::: ## Exercise 3 {.exercise} * Calculate the CDI for the PIE HDCM model * What conclusions or recommendations might we make based on these results? {{< countdown "2:00" bottom="15%" >}} ## {.exercise data-menu-title="Solution 3"} ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} cdi(pie_hdcm) #> $item_discrimination #> # A tibble: 15 × 5 #> task overall L1 L2 L3 #> #> 1 00592 0.124 0.737 0 0 #> 2 14415 0.108 0.545 0 0 #> 3 56400 0.0358 0.152 0 0 #> 4 64967 0.0162 0.0760 0 0 #> 5 06238 0.0974 0 0.175 0 #> 6 10231 0.0613 0 0.110 0 #> 7 54596 0.0224 0 0.0403 0 #> 8 96748 0.148 0 0.267 0 #> 9 97634 0.102 0 0.183 0 #> 10 13080 0.631 0 0 1.11 #> 11 27971 1.17 0 0 2.12 #> 12 56741 0.389 0 0 0.699 #> 13 63088 1.90 0 0 3.53 #> 14 81175 0.599 0 0 1.04 #> 15 88063 0.234 0 0 0.395 #> #> $test_discrimination #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #> overall L1 L2 L3 #> #> 1 5.63 1.51 0.776 8.90 ``` ::: ## Q-matrix validation * The Q-matrix defines which items measure each attribute * Is the Q-matrix correct? * Are there other Q-matrices that might better explain the observed data * measr implements a general Q-matrix validation measure from [de la Torre & Chiu (2016)](https://doi.org/10.1007/s11336-015-9467-8) * Assumes that the set of attributes is correct * Does not evaluate whether additional, fewer, or different attributes would be appropriate ## Q-matrix validation with measr ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-line-numbers="|13"} qmatrix_validation(ecpe_lcdm) #> # A tibble: 28 × 5 #> item_id original_specification original_pvaf empirical_specification #> #> 1 E1 [1, 1, 0] 0.995 #> 2 E2 [0, 1, 0] 0.989 #> 3 E3 [1, 0, 1] 0.996 #> 4 E4 [0, 0, 1] 0.980 #> 5 E5 [0, 0, 1] 0.993 #> 6 E6 [0, 0, 1] 0.980 #> 7 E7 [1, 0, 1] 1.000 #> 8 E8 [0, 1, 0] 0.986 #> 9 E9 [0, 0, 1] 0.925 [1, 0, 1] #> 10 E10 [1, 0, 0] 0.983 #> # ℹ 18 more rows #> # ℹ 1 more variable: empirical_pvaf ``` ::: ## Exercise 4 {.exercise} * Check the Q-matrix for the PIE HDCM * Are there any items that may measure additional pathway levels? {{< countdown "2:00" bottom="15%" >}} ## {.exercise data-menu-title="Solution 4"} ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} qmatrix_validation(pie_hdcm) #> # A tibble: 15 × 5 #> task original_specification original_pvaf empirical_specification #> #> 1 00592 [1, 0, 0] 0.450 [1, 0, 1] #> 2 14415 [1, 0, 0] 0.580 [1, 0, 1] #> 3 56400 [1, 0, 0] 0.699 [1, 0, 1] #> 4 64967 [1, 0, 0] 0.150 [0, 1, 1] #> 5 06238 [0, 1, 0] 0.522 [1, 1, 1] #> 6 10231 [0, 1, 0] 0.551 [0, 1, 1] #> 7 54596 [0, 1, 0] 0.470 [0, 1, 1] #> 8 96748 [0, 1, 0] 0.875 [1, 1, 1] #> 9 97634 [0, 1, 0] 0.461 [0, 1, 1] #> 10 13080 [0, 0, 1] 0.997 #> 11 27971 [0, 0, 1] 1.000 #> 12 56741 [0, 0, 1] 0.996 #> 13 63088 [0, 0, 1] 1.000 #> 14 81175 [0, 0, 1] 1.000 #> 15 88063 [0, 0, 1] 1.000 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: empirical_pvaf ``` ::: # {.closing data-menu-title="Closing"}
:::{.end-title color="white" font-size="200%"} Model misspecifications ::: :::{.end-subtitle} Attributes and items ::: :::{.center} :::

include-after-body:
<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
